calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For
Dismissal And Order Thereon
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITIBANK, N.A.'s Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Dismissal And Order Thereon.

Plaintiff Motion to Set Aside Judgment and for Dismissal and Order Thereon is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.

Good cause appearing, the court sets aside and vacates the 2/2/2024 default and default judgment entered in this action.

The court grants Plaintiff leave to dismiss the action without prejudice. Plaintiff shall present an executed CIV-110 to the Clerk to accomplish dismissal.

To the extent Plaintiff requests different or additional relief, those requests are denied.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For
Dismissal And Order Thereon
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Continued To May-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Motion to Set Aside Judgment and for Dismissal and Order Thereon is CONINTUED TO MAY 7, 2026, AND RELATED ORDERS ARE ISSUED.

On the court's own motion, the court strikes the post-judgment dismissal without prejudice entered by the court on 3/25/2026. Plaintiff's request for dismissal was wholly improper and the clerk erred in entering a dismissal pursuant to Plaintiff's improper request. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 581.)

On 2/19/2026 and 2/20/2026, the court ordered Plaintiff to timely submit a proposed order verbatim the tentative ruling for the 2/20/2026 hearing. Plaintiff is in violation of that order. The court, again, orders Plaintiff to submit a proposed order for the 2/20/2026 hearing, verbatim the tentative ruling for that hearing, which has been adopted. Plaintiff must do so no later than 4/9/2026 at 8:30 am and shall lodge the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

On 2/20/2026, the court ordered as follows: "If another motion seeking the same or substantially identical relief [is filed], the court will require service on Defendant." The instant motion is one seeking the same or substantially identical relief. Plaintiff has been ordered to serve Defendant, who is represented. Yet Plaintiff refuses. Plaintiff's contention that it need not serve Defendant fails on the facts of this case. Plaintiff served Defendant with a substantially identical motion in December 2025 and the court has ordered that it serve this motion on Defendant. Moreover, Plaintiff seeks relief based on an agreement with Defendant. Defendant may want to be heard, which is not possible if she has not been served and provided notice.

The court is continuing the hearing to allow for timely service of the moving papers on Defendant. Plaintiff shall include a cover sheet identifying the new hearing date.

Prior to the hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim. Plaintiff must promptly serve the order from the 2/20/2026 hearing and this hearing on Defendant and file proper proof of doing so in the register of actions.

The Court signs two orders. ((see scanned orders for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Dismissal And Order Thereon
Off Calendar Feb-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Citibank, N.A.'s Motion to Set Aside Judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Plaintiff's motion identifies no legal basis for the requested order. Nor does the motion identify a legitimate factual basis for the requested order. "My client asked me to" is not a legitimate basis.

If another motion seeking the same or substantially identical relief is filed, Plaintiff must identify a legitimate factual basis for granting the proposed order and a legal basis. The court does not understand the law to give it unrestricted discretion to vacate judgments. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 473, et seq.) Additionally, the court will require service on Defendant. Plaintiff served Defendant with its 12/19/2025 motion through counsel. Plaintiff must serve any subsequent motion, as well.

Prior to the hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Dismissal And Order Thereon
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Continued To Feb-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITIBANK, N.A.'s Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Dismissal And Order Thereon.

Continued on the court's own motion to be heard on February 20, 2026.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion For Set Aside Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Citibank, N.A.'s Motion For [sic] Set Aside Judgment is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff served this motion on December 18, 2025, filed it on December 19, 2025, and noticed it for hearing on January 8, 2026. Service and filing were untimely. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address or place of mailing is within State of California adds 5 calendar days to "any period of notice"]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Further, the motion is unsupported by any memorandum or evidence.

Plaintiff may have intended to advance the January 22, 2026 hearing on a differently titled motion. The court will not infer this, given the different titles. In any event, the effort to advance fails because the notice was not timely served or filed. The January 22, 2026 hearing remains on calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
